images.caption:
Figure 1:Witharia, the same Stable Audio 3 checkpoints move from a
framework-bound serving stack (top) to a single dependency-free binary on hardware
people own (bottom):7×7\timesfaster cold start, warm parity, and both model
variants on a Raspberry Pi 5. Owning the pipeline also makes activation steering a runtime
feature, injected at1the DiT residual stream,2the latent, or3the text
conditioning.

Figure 2:The degradation demonstration (sour, small-music, its best layer L19), as two
stacked panels sharingα\alpha(no dual axis).Top:the steering target (wav2tasteΔ\Delta,
with a95%95\%bootstrap CI band over the paired per-clip deltas) and the independent CLAPΔ\Deltaon one axis; both rise inside the shaded low-α\alphagenuine window, then diverge as wav2taste stays
high while CLAP collapses.Bottom:FAD explodes past the window. The high-α\alphawav2taste
gain is degradation that games the oracle. Colours are the fixed colourblind-safe taste palette
(CLAP/FAD are shown neutral, being independent checks rather than tastes).

Figure 3:Every DiT layer steered individually atα=0.15\alpha{=}0.15(small-music;n=15n{=}15clips
per point).sweetpeaks mid-late (L16) and collapses at the final blocks, while the four
intense tastes spike at the final layer, a ranking the quality control later overturns as
degradation (their genuine site is the shaded L14–17). Medium behaves alike, peaking at
L20–21.

Figure 4:Quantization performance in one space (small-music; all GPU device-resident, fp16 decoder,
so only DiT precision differs). Quantizing slides a model left (less Raspberry-Pi memory) at a
fidelity cost (up: FAD vs. the fp16 set); marker area grows with the wav2taste taste-vector L2. The
shaded band is the FAD a mere fp16 re-seed induces. 8-bit (q8, W8A8) stays inside the band and small,
and within theΔ\DeltaCLAP and taste floors too (TableII), so it is indistinguishable
from fp16; W8A8 is also the fastest GPU mode. 4-bit leaves the band for little extra memory but is
what runs the1.21.2B medium model on an88GB Pi.
